name: Kunal Shah
headline: Founder, CRED | Co-founder, FreeCharge
how_to_reach: Twitter DMs (@kunalb11); warm intro via CRED or Sequoia network preferred
investment_thesis: Products that deliver 'delta-4' efficiency gains; FinTech infrastructure; consumer behaviour change; businesses targeting aspirational India
bio: Kunal Shah is the founder of CRED, a members-only credit card bill payment and rewards platform. Previously co-founded FreeCharge, which was acquired by Snapdeal in 2015 for ~$400M. One of India's most active and visible angel investors, known for his framework of 'delta-4 products'—those that increase efficiency by at least 4x. Has backed 100+ startups and is a prolific thought leader on Indian consumer behaviour.

name: Anupam Mittal
headline: Founder, Shaadi.com | Shark Tank India Judge
current_role: Founder & CEO, People Group (Shaadi.com, Mauj Mobile, Shayari.com)
how_to_reach: Shark Tank India applications; Twitter DMs; warm intro via Mumbai Angels or People Group network
investment_thesis: Contrarian bets on founders with deep domain expertise; consumer internet; mobile-first products; businesses with strong network effects
bio: Anupam Mittal founded Shaadi.com in 1997, one of India's earliest internet companies and the world's largest matrimony platform. A Shark Tank India (Season 1–4) judge and one of the country's most prominent angels, he has invested in 200+ startups over two decades. Known for backing founders early and being a hands-on mentor.

name: Ronnie Screwvala
headline: Founder, UTV Group | Founder, upGrad
notable_exits_or_companies: UTV Software Communications (sold to Disney for ~$454M, 2012)
how_to_reach: Warm intro via upGrad or Unilazer Ventures network; LinkedIn
investment_thesis: Transformational businesses in EdTech, media, healthcare, and sports; strong brand-building capability; large market potential in India
bio: Ronnie Screwvala built UTV Group into one of India's largest media and entertainment companies before selling it to Disney. He then co-founded upGrad, India's leading online higher-education platform. Invests selectively through Unilazer Ventures with a focus on large, disruptive opportunities in India.

name: Nithin Kamath
headline: Founder & CEO, Zerodha
notable_exits_or_companies: Zerodha (India's largest stockbroker, bootstrapped to $2B+ valuation)
how_to_reach: Twitter (@Nithin0dha); warm intro via Zerodha/Rainmatter network; apply to Rainmatter Capital (sister fund)
investment_thesis: Capital markets infrastructure; wealth management; financial literacy; frugal businesses with strong unit economics; impact investing
bio: Nithin Kamath co-founded Zerodha in 2010, bootstrapping it to become India's largest retail stockbroker with 10M+ active clients. He invests personally and through Rainmatter Capital in FinTech and capital-market-adjacent startups. Known for his frugality-first mindset and deep expertise in trading and retail investor behaviour.

name: Nikhil Kamath
headline: Co-founder, Zerodha & True Beacon | Founder, WTF Fund
current_role: Co-founder, Zerodha; Founder, WTF Fund; Founder, Gruhas
how_to_reach: WTF Fund applications (wtffund.in); warm intro; Twitter DMs
investment_thesis: Early-stage contrarian bets; real estate tech (via Gruhas); creator economy; disruptive FinTech; founder-centric approach
known_investments: Various early-stage FinTech and consumer startups
bio: Nikhil Kamath co-founded Zerodha alongside his brother Nithin and is one of India's youngest billionaires. He runs True Beacon (an alternative asset management firm), WTF Fund (an early-stage fund for young founders), and Gruhas (a PropTech-focused investment vehicle). Known for his contrarian views and heavy Twitter presence.

name: Deepinder Goyal
headline: Founder & CEO, Zomato
notable_exits_or_companies: Zomato (NSE/BSE listed, $20B+ market cap at peak)
how_to_reach: Warm intro via Zomato/Info Edge ecosystem; LinkedIn
investment_thesis: Consumer-first businesses; food and grocery; quick commerce; strong operational teams; India-at-scale opportunities
bio: Deepinder Goyal founded Zomato in 2008 (originally Foodiebay) from an IIT Delhi campus project into one of India's most iconic internet companies. Zomato listed on Indian stock exchanges in 2021 and has since pivoted into quick commerce via Blinkit. Deepinder invests selectively in high-growth consumer businesses.

name: Aadit Palicha
headline: Co-founder & CEO, Zepto
how_to_reach: Twitter DMs; warm intro via Zepto or Y Combinator alumni network
investment_thesis: Operationally intense businesses; quick commerce; consumer tech; deep tech with India applicability; young audacious founders
known_investments: Various early-stage consumer and quick-commerce startups
bio: Aadit Palicha co-founded Zepto in 2021 at age 19 (a Stanford dropout) alongside Kaivalya Vohra, creating India's leading 10-minute grocery delivery service. At 23, he is among India's youngest unicorn founders and has begun making angel investments in audacious early-stage startups.

name: Girish Mathrubootham
headline: Founder, Freshworks | SaaS Pioneer
notable_exits_or_companies: Freshworks (NASDAQ listed: FRSH, IPO 2021, $12B+ peak market cap)
how_to_reach: Warm intro via Freshworks or SaaS community; LinkedIn
investment_thesis: Global SaaS built from India; strong product teams; customer-centric culture; businesses solving real enterprise pain points at scale
bio: Girish Mathrubootham founded Freshworks (originally Freshdesk) in 2010 in Chennai, bootstrapping it to a successful NASDAQ IPO in 2021—one of the largest SaaS IPOs of the year. A champion of India's SaaS ecosystem, he actively mentors and invests in B2B SaaS companies built by Indian founders for global markets.

name: Mekin Maheshwari
headline: Ex-CPO & Head of People, Flipkart | Founder, Udhyam Learning
notable_exits_or_companies: Flipkart (sold to Walmart for $16B, 2018 — as early employee/executive)
how_to_reach: LinkedIn; warm intro via Flipkart Mafia network; Udhyam Learning connections
investment_thesis: Founders building for Bharat; EdTech for employability; future of work; social impact businesses that are also commercially viable
bio: Mekin Maheshwari was one of Flipkart's earliest and most influential executives, serving as CPO and Head of People. After Walmart's acquisition, he founded Udhyam Learning Foundation to build entrepreneurial mindsets in youth. He is one of the Flipkart Mafia's most prolific angels, with a strong thesis around Bharat-first startups.

name: Sriharsha Majety
headline: Co-founder & CEO, Swiggy
notable_exits_or_companies: Swiggy (NSE/BSE listed, IPO 2024, valued ~$11.3B at listing)
how_to_reach: Warm intro via Swiggy or Accel network; LinkedIn
investment_thesis: Execution-heavy consumer businesses; hyperlocal delivery; supply chain; logistics innovation; founders who can build and scale ops
known_investments: Various consumer and logistics startups in early rounds
bio: Sriharsha Majety co-founded Swiggy in 2014, building it from a Bengaluru-based food delivery startup into one of India's largest consumer internet platforms before a landmark IPO in 2024. He invests selectively in founders building operationally complex, execution-intensive businesses.

name: Sachin Bansal
headline: Co-founder, Flipkart | Founder, Navi Technologies
how_to_reach: Warm intro via Flipkart Mafia or Navi Technologies network
investment_thesis: Large market FinTech opportunities in India; consumer credit and insurance; e-commerce infrastructure; bold founders targeting financial inclusion
bio: Sachin Bansal co-founded Flipkart, India's first e-commerce unicorn, with Binny Bansal in 2007. After Walmart's $16B acquisition in 2018, he exited and founded Navi Technologies, a FinTech company offering loans, insurance, and mutual funds. He has been a substantial early-stage backer of many Indian consumer internet companies.

name: Binny Bansal
headline: Co-founder, Flipkart | Founder, xto10x Technologies
how_to_reach: Warm intro via xto10x, Flipkart Mafia, or 021 Capital network
investment_thesis: Scale-up stage companies needing operational and strategic mentorship; B2B SaaS; companies that need help scaling from $10M to $100M ARR
bio: Binny Bansal co-founded Flipkart with Sachin Bansal in 2007, serving as CEO after Sachin stepped down. Post-Walmart acquisition, he launched xto10x Technologies to help startups scale operations. Invests through 021 Capital and personal capacity in high-growth startups.

name: Nandan Nilekani
headline: Co-founder, Infosys | Architect of Aadhaar & UPI
how_to_reach: Warm intro via Infosys, EkStep Foundation, or iSPIRT network; LinkedIn
investment_thesis: Digital public infrastructure layered businesses; Account Aggregator ecosystem; ONDC; Open Credit Enablement Network; financial inclusion at scale; India Stack opportunities
bio: Nandan Nilekani co-founded Infosys and architected India's digital identity (Aadhaar) and payments (UPI) infrastructure as UIDAI chairman. He co-founded EkStep Foundation and has been instrumental in building India Stack. His investments focus on businesses that leverage India's digital public infrastructure layers.

name: Mohandas Pai
headline: Ex-CFO, Infosys | Chairman, Aarin Capital
current_role: Chairman, Aarin Capital; Chairman, Manipal Global Education
notable_exits_or_companies: Infosys (as CFO and Board member during peak growth years)
how_to_reach: Aarin Capital application; warm intro via Manipal or Infosys ecosystem; LinkedIn
investment_thesis: Education and healthcare transformation in India; deep tech; SaaS for global markets; building businesses that create large-scale employment in India
bio: Mohandas Pai was Infosys's CFO and a pivotal figure in its global expansion. He chairs Aarin Capital (an early-stage fund) and Manipal Global Education. One of Bengaluru's most well-connected investors and a vocal voice on startup policy. Known for both his prolific investments and outspoken views on taxation and regulation.

name: Pravin Jadhav
headline: Founder, Raise Financial | Ex-CEO, Paytm Money
how_to_reach: Twitter DMs (@pravindj); LinkedIn; warm intro via WealthTech community
investment_thesis: Democratising investing and wealth creation for middle India; WealthTech infrastructure; capital markets technology; frugal founders with deep FinTech expertise
known_investments: Various WealthTech and FinTech seed-stage startups
bio: Pravin Jadhav co-founded Wishberry, India's first rewards crowdfunding platform, and later led Paytm Money as its CEO. He founded Raise Financial to simplify stock investing for retail Indians. Active angel in the FinTech and WealthTech space with a focus on bridging India's investing gap.

name: Avnish Bajaj
headline: Co-founder & MD, Matrix Partners India | Founder, Baazee.com
current_role: Co-founder & Managing Director, Matrix Partners India
how_to_reach: Matrix Partners India application (matrixpartners.in); warm intro via Matrix portfolio
investment_thesis: Founder-first approach; category-defining companies in large markets; strong product/technology moat; businesses that can scale to $1B+ outcomes
bio: Avnish Bajaj founded Baazee.com, India's first online marketplace, sold to eBay in 2004. He then co-founded Matrix Partners India, one of the country's top venture funds. While primarily a VC, he also makes personal angel bets. Known for backing contrarian founders and complex market opportunities.

name: Kunal Bahl
headline: Co-founder, Snapdeal & Titan Capital
current_role: Co-founder, Snapdeal; Co-founder & General Partner, Titan Capital
notable_exits_or_companies: Snapdeal (India's largest marketplace before Flipkart competition; FreeCharge acquisition for ~$400M resold to Axis Bank)
how_to_reach: Titan Capital application; Twitter DMs; warm intro via Snapdeal or Delhi NCR startup community
investment_thesis: First-time founders with obsessive customer focus; strong fundamentals and unit economics; early-stage India-first consumer and B2B opportunities
bio: Kunal Bahl co-founded Snapdeal in 2010, scaling it to a $6.5B valuation at its peak. He and co-founder Rohit Bansal set up Titan Capital, one of India's most prolific early-stage angel funds. With 200+ investments, Titan Capital is known for backing India's most successful D2C and consumer brands.

name: Vijay Shekhar Sharma
headline: Founder & CEO, Paytm (One97 Communications)
how_to_reach: Warm intro via Paytm or One97 network; LinkedIn
investment_thesis: Payments infrastructure; FinTech for the masses; mobile-first India; businesses leveraging UPI and digital payments growth
known_investments: Various early-stage FinTech and consumer internet companies
bio: Vijay Shekhar Sharma founded Paytm (One97 Communications) in 2000, turning it into India's largest digital payments platform. Paytm's 2021 IPO was India's largest at the time (₹18,300 crore). Born in Aligarh, UP, VSS is one of India's most recognizable entrepreneurs and makes selective angel bets.

name: Amit Agarwal
headline: VP & Country Manager, Amazon India | First Employee, Amazon
current_role: Former SVP Global Selling, Amazon; Board Member and Investor
notable_exits_or_companies: Amazon India (built from scratch to $6B+ revenue as country head)
how_to_reach: Warm intro via Amazon India alumni network; LinkedIn
investment_thesis: E-commerce and logistics infrastructure; enterprise SaaS; supply chain innovation; deep tech with global scale potential
bio: Amit Agarwal was Amazon's first employee in India and built Amazon.in into one of the country's largest e-commerce platforms as VP and Country Manager. A global executive who understands both startup hustle and enterprise scale, he backs founders building the next layer of India's commerce infrastructure.

name: Alok Goel
headline: Ex-CEO, Freecharge | Ex-Google India | MD, SAIF Partners
notable_exits_or_companies: FreeCharge (as CEO during $400M sale to Snapdeal)
how_to_reach: Elevation Capital application (elevationcapital.com); warm intro via Elevation portfolio
investment_thesis: Mobile-first consumer businesses; FinTech; EdTech; companies building for the next 500M Indian internet users; strong product culture
bio: Alok Goel led Google India's business development before becoming CEO of FreeCharge and overseeing its sale. He joined SAIF Partners (rebranded Elevation Capital) as MD and has been a prolific early-stage investor. Known for his deep product expertise and focus on India's mass market.

name: Sanjay Mehta
headline: Founder & GP, 100X.VC | Pioneer of iSAFE in India
notable_exits_or_companies: 100X.VC (India's first fund using iSAFE notes for angel investments)
how_to_reach: 100X.VC application (100x.vc); warm intro preferred; cohort-based selection
investment_thesis: Backing 100 startups per year using iSAFE notes; diverse sectors; first-time founders with strong execution ability; startups that can grow 100x
known_investments: 100+ companies per batch; portfolio includes multiple unicorns and soonicorns
bio: Sanjay Mehta is the founder of 100X.VC, which pioneered the use of iSAFE (India Simple Agreement for Future Equity) notes for angel investing. He has backed 200+ startups across multiple cohorts, making him one of India's most prolific individual angels. Runs a cohort model, selecting startups twice a year.

name: Phanindra Sama
headline: Co-founder, redBus | Ex-CEO, NSDC
current_role: Co-founder & CEO, Chalo (mobility startup); Angel Investor
how_to_reach: LinkedIn; warm intro via redBus alumni or Bengaluru startup community
investment_thesis: Solving real India problems in mobility and logistics; technology for mass transit; social impact businesses with commercial viability; Bharat-focused founders
known_investments: Various mobility and SaaS seed-stage startups
bio: Phanindra Sama co-founded redBus in 2006—India's first online bus ticketing platform—from an IIT alumni basement. Sold to ibibo/Naspers in 2013. He later served as CEO of NSDC (National Skill Development Corporation) and co-founded Chalo, a mobility platform for city buses. An active angel in the Hyderabad-Bengaluru corridor.

name: Pallav Nadhani
headline: Co-founder, FusionCharts | Prolific SaaS Angel
notable_exits_or_companies: FusionCharts (sold to Idera Inc, 2019; bootstrapped to $10M+ ARR)
how_to_reach: Twitter DMs; LinkedIn; warm intro via SaaS community
investment_thesis: B2B SaaS built for global markets from India; developer tools; data and analytics; bootstrappable businesses with path to profitability
bio: Pallav Nadhani co-founded FusionCharts in 2002 at age 17, bootstrapping it to become the world's leading JavaScript charting library used by 28,000+ companies. After the Idera acquisition in 2019, he became one of India's most respected SaaS angels, backing dozens of early-stage B2B companies.

name: Naval Ravikant
headline: Co-founder, AngelList | Philosopher-Investor
current_role: Chairman Emeritus, AngelList; Angel Investor; Podcaster
notable_exits_or_companies: AngelList (built the global angel investing ecosystem)
how_to_reach: AngelList profile; extremely selective — typically invests only through strong warm introductions
investment_thesis: Wealth creation through leverage (code, capital, media); network effects; Web3 and crypto infrastructure; AI; companies that can scale without linear human effort
bio: Naval Ravikant is of Indian origin and co-founded AngelList, the platform that democratized angel investing globally. A philosopher, podcaster, and prolific investor, his 'How to Get Rich' tweetstorm influenced an entire generation of Indian founders. While US-based, he remains deeply connected to the Indian startup diaspora.

name: Ratan Tata
headline: Chairman Emeritus, Tata Sons | Iconic Indian Industrialist
current_role: Chairman Emeritus, Tata Sons (Passed away October 2024)
how_to_reach: N/A — passed away in October 2024; legacy investments through RNT Capital Advisors
investment_thesis: Brand-building for India; social impact combined with commercial scale; manufacturing excellence; EV and sustainability; empowering underserved communities
bio: Ratan Tata, who passed away in October 2024, was India's most beloved industrialist. Under his leadership, Tata Group transformed from a Rs 10,000 crore to Rs 10 lakh crore conglomerate. In his personal capacity, he backed 50+ startups over the last decade, with a special fondness for social impact and brand-driven businesses. His involvement was considered a badge of honour for Indian startups.

name: Azim Premji
headline: Founder, Wipro | Founder, Azim Premji Foundation
current_role: Founder & Chairman, Azim Premji Foundation; Former Executive Chairman, Wipro
how_to_reach: Azim Premji Philanthropic Initiatives; Premji Invest (family office); warm intro through Wipro ecosystem
investment_thesis: Education and healthcare for Bharat; social impact with long-term thinking; technology for underserved communities; sustainable business models with impact at scale
known_investments: Premji Invest portfolio (Byju's early, Swiggy early, Xiaomi India, Delhivery)
bio: Azim Premji transformed Wipro from a vegetable oil company into a global IT giant. Pledging 67%+ of his wealth to philanthropy, he established the Azim Premji Foundation (education) and Premji Invest. Among India's largest philanthropists. Investments combine returns with deep social impact conviction.

name: Sunil Munjal
headline: Chairman, Hero Enterprise | Promoter, Hero Group
notable_exits_or_companies: Hero Honda (split; Hero MotoCorp listed with $9B+ market cap)
how_to_reach: Warm intro via Hero Enterprise or Delhi NCR industrial network; LinkedIn
investment_thesis: India's manufacturing resurgence; EV revolution; financial services for Bharat; consumer brands with scale potential; family-business-to-startup transitions
bio: Sunil Munjal is the co-promoter and strategic architect of the Hero Group, one of India's largest conglomerates with Hero MotoCorp at its core. Through Hero Enterprise, he leads diversified investments across financial services, retail, and tech-enabled businesses. A respected voice on Indian manufacturing and entrepreneurship.

name: Manoj Kohli
headline: Ex-MD, SoftBank India | Ex-CEO, Bharti Airtel
current_role: Independent Director; Startup Advisor; Angel Investor
notable_exits_or_companies: Bharti Airtel (as MD/CEO International; grew from 2M to 200M subscribers)
how_to_reach: LinkedIn; warm intro via SoftBank India or Airtel network
investment_thesis: Telecom-adjacent tech; financial inclusion; digital health; enterprise SaaS; businesses that can scale across Asia and Africa leveraging India expertise
known_investments: Various telecom tech and enterprise SaaS startups
bio: Manoj Kohli spent 25 years at Bharti Airtel as its MD/CEO (International), expanding it across 18 African countries. He then led SoftBank's India operations as Country Head. Now an active angel and board member, he brings a rare mix of telecom depth and emerging market expertise to his portfolio companies.

name: Varun Dua
headline: Co-founder & CEO, Acko Insurance
how_to_reach: Twitter DMs; warm intro via Acko or InsurTech community; LinkedIn
investment_thesis: InsurTech and digital financial products; embedded insurance; consumer FinTech; data-driven underwriting; health and wellness businesses
known_investments: Various InsurTech and FinTech seed-stage startups
bio: Varun Dua co-founded Acko in 2016, building India's first digital-native insurance company. Acko reached unicorn status in 2021 by offering micro-insurance and embedded insurance products. Varun is an active angel in the InsurTech and broader FinTech ecosystem, bringing deep regulatory and product knowledge.

name: Gaurav Munjal
headline: Co-founder & CEO, Unacademy
how_to_reach: Twitter DMs (@gauravmunjal); warm intro via Unacademy or General Catalyst network
investment_thesis: EdTech and education infrastructure; creator economy; D2C consumer brands; mobile-first products for aspirational India
known_investments: Various EdTech and consumer early-stage startups; prolific supporter of IIT/IIM founder networks
bio: Gaurav Munjal co-founded Unacademy, originally a YouTube channel with free learning content, scaling it into India's largest ed-learning platform with 50M+ learners. An active angel, he invests in early-stage EdTech and consumer products, drawing on his experience building Unacademy's educator and learner ecosystems.

name: Saikiran Krishnamurthy
headline: Former MD, Temasek India | Startup Ecosystem Builder
current_role: Independent Investor; Board Member; Startup Advisor
notable_exits_or_companies: Temasek India investments: Flipkart, OYO, Zomato, PolicyBazaar (as key investment decision-maker)
how_to_reach: LinkedIn; warm intro via Temasek or Bengaluru startup ecosystem
investment_thesis: Disciplined growth businesses; strong governance; businesses building durable competitive moats; Southeast Asia-India crossover opportunities
known_investments: Personal bets in enterprise SaaS and consumer internet early-rounds
bio: Saikiran Krishnamurthy was the Managing Director of Temasek India, overseeing the sovereign wealth fund's technology and consumer investments in the country. Under his tenure, Temasek backed several of India's largest unicorns. Now an independent investor and board member bringing institutional-grade diligence to angel investments.

name: Jasminder Singh Gulati
headline: Co-founder, UrbanClap (Urban Company)
current_role: Co-founder & Chief Growth Officer, Urban Company
how_to_reach: LinkedIn; warm intro via Urban Company network; Delhi NCR founder community
investment_thesis: Marketplace businesses with supply-side training and quality control; home services; consumer platforms; gig economy with worker welfare focus
bio: Jasminder Singh Gulati co-founded UrbanClap (now Urban Company) in 2014, building it into India's largest platform for home services. Urban Company reached unicorn status in 2021 and expanded to the UAE and Australia. Jas invests in marketplace and consumer businesses where he can add operational value.

name: Mohit Satyanand
headline: Chairman, Chennai Angels | Serial Entrepreneur & Angel
current_role: Chairman, Chennai Angels; Managing Director, Tangerine Arts & Health
notable_exits_or_companies: Multiple entrepreneurial ventures in media and health across 30+ year career
how_to_reach: Chennai Angels application; LinkedIn; Southern India startup community events
investment_thesis: Wellness and preventive healthcare; education innovation; media and culture businesses; social enterprises with revenue models; South India-rooted founders
known_investments: Chennai Angels portfolio: 50+ companies across health, ed, and consumer sectors
bio: Mohit Satyanand is a serial entrepreneur who chairs the Chennai Angels, one of South India's most active angel networks. He has backed 50+ companies over two decades with a special interest in wellness, preventive health, and education. A thought leader in the Chennai startup ecosystem.

name: Anil Joshi
headline: Managing Partner, Unicorn India Ventures
notable_exits_or_companies: Unicorn India Ventures portfolio exits across enterprise SaaS and IoT
how_to_reach: Unicorn India Ventures application; LinkedIn; Mumbai/Pune founder ecosystem
investment_thesis: Enterprise SaaS with global go-to-market; IoT for industrial and smart city applications; FinTech infrastructure; deep tech with defensible IP
bio: Anil Joshi is the managing partner of Unicorn India Ventures, a Mumbai-based early-stage fund that has backed 60+ companies. He brings deep operational expertise from his corporate career and a focus on enterprise technology and deep tech. Known for patient capital and founder-friendly approach.

name: Abhay Bhargava
headline: Founder & Managing Partner, WEH Ventures
notable_exits_or_companies: Multiple corporate roles including Frost & Sullivan leadership across Asia
how_to_reach: WEH Ventures application; LinkedIn; warm intro via impact investing community
investment_thesis: Climate and clean energy tech; impact investing with market returns; agri-food innovation; health for tier-2 and tier-3 India; water and sanitation technology
known_investments: WEH Ventures portfolio across cleantech, agritech, and health sectors in India
bio: Abhay Bhargava founded WEH Ventures (Where Every Human thrives), an early-stage impact fund focused on sustainability and social good. Previously a senior leader at Frost & Sullivan across Asia. He is one of India's leading voices on impact investing and backs startups solving deep India challenges at the intersection of business and environment.

name: Rajan Anandan
headline: MD, Peak XV Partners (ex-Sequoia) | Ex-VP Google India & SEA
current_role: Managing Director, Peak XV Partners (formerly Sequoia Capital India & SEA)
notable_exits_or_companies: Google India (built from 200 to 2,000 employees as VP & MD)
how_to_reach: Peak XV Partners application; warm intro via Peak XV or Google India network
investment_thesis: India and Southeast Asia's next generation of internet-first businesses; EdTech, FinTech, SaaS; companies building for the next billion users; strong product-market fit evidence
bio: Rajan Anandan was VP & MD of Google India, transforming it into a dominant force across search, YouTube, and cloud. He joined Sequoia Capital India (now Peak XV) and launched Surge, a rapid-scale program for startups in India and Southeast Asia. One of India's most networked and respected investors.

name: Vani Kola
headline: Founder & MD, Kalaari Capital | Pioneer VC in India
notable_exits_or_companies: Two Silicon Valley startups (Corelink and RightWorks, both acquired) before returning to India
how_to_reach: Kalaari Capital application (kalaari.com); warm intro via Kalaari portfolio
investment_thesis: India-first consumer businesses; healthcare transformation; women-led entrepreneurship; social commerce; bottom-of-pyramid inclusivity alongside premium consumer plays
bio: Vani Kola founded Kalaari Capital in 2006, making her one of India's first professional venture capitalists and the most prominent woman VC in the ecosystem. She built two successful US tech companies before returning to India. Known for her founder empathy, she has been an early backer of some of India's most successful startups.

name: Rahul Mehta
headline: Managing Partner, DST Global | Early Backer of Global Tech Giants
how_to_reach: DST Global (dst.global); typically invests at growth stages; warm intro via global VC network required
investment_thesis: Category-defining consumer internet companies globally; high-growth marketplaces; dominant FinTech platforms; companies with defensible network effects at scale
bio: Rahul Mehta is a Managing Partner at DST Global, the legendary growth-stage technology fund known for backing Facebook, Twitter, Spotify, and Airbnb early. With Indian roots, he has been instrumental in DST's major bets on Indian unicorns including Flipkart, Swiggy, and BYJU's.

name: Shashin Shah
headline: Founder, THINK9 Global | Serial Entrepreneur & Angel
current_role: Founder & CEO, THINK9 Global (D2C brand building platform)
notable_exits_or_companies: Multiple D2C brand exits through THINK9 platform
how_to_reach: LinkedIn; warm intro via D2C community or Gujarat startup ecosystem
investment_thesis: D2C brands with strong supply chain; consumer product innovation; FMCG alternatives; Gujarat and Tier-2 city-originated founders with national ambitions
known_investments: Various D2C and consumer brand early-stage companies
bio: Shashin Shah founded THINK9 Global, a platform that builds, scales, and exits D2C consumer brands. An active angel in the D2C space, he brings deep expertise in brand building, supply chain management, and e-commerce growth. Strong connector in the Ahmedabad and Gujarat entrepreneurial community.

name: Padmaja Ruparel
headline: Co-founder, Indian Angel Network | Pioneer of Angel Investing in India
current_role: Co-founder & President, IAN Group (Indian Angel Network + IAN Fund)
notable_exits_or_companies: IAN portfolio exits: Druva, Box8, Healthkart, Sapience Analytics
how_to_reach: IAN application (indianangelnetwork.com); LinkedIn; warm intro via IAN network
investment_thesis: First-check investor for India's most promising early-stage startups; diverse sectors with India applicability; women entrepreneurs; grassroots innovation
known_investments: 300+ companies via IAN over 18 years; Druva, Box8, Healthkart notable exits
bio: Padmaja Ruparel co-founded the Indian Angel Network in 2006, building India's first and largest angel investor network. She has personally backed 300+ startups and is one of the country's most influential early-stage investors. A champion of gender diversity in entrepreneurship, she has helped put Indian angel investing on the global map.

name: Ashish Kumar Singh
headline: Co-founder, Incubate Fund India | Top-100 Angel Investor in Asia
notable_exits_or_companies: Multiple early-stage exits including SaaS and consumer companies
how_to_reach: Incubate Fund India application; LinkedIn; warm intro via Japan-India startup community
investment_thesis: India-Japan corridor opportunities; early-stage SaaS for global markets; Japanese enterprise entry into India; AI and robotics businesses
known_investments: 80+ companies across Incubate Fund India portfolio
bio: Ashish Kumar Singh co-founded Incubate Fund India, a seed-stage fund bridging the Japan-India startup ecosystem. Recognized as a Top-100 Angel Investor in Asia, he has backed 80+ companies. Brings unique access to Japanese enterprise partnerships for Indian startups.

name: Sanjeev Bikhchandani
headline: Founder, Info Edge (Naukri.com, 99acres, Jeevansathi)
notable_exits_or_companies: Info Edge (NSE/BSE listed, $5B+ market cap); Naukri.com (market leader in online jobs)
how_to_reach: Info Edge/Makesense application; LinkedIn; warm intro via Info Edge network
investment_thesis: Internet businesses with strong fundamentals and patient capital approach; marketplaces with strong network effects; category leaders in large Indian verticals
bio: Sanjeev Bikhchandani founded Naukri.com in 1997, one of India's oldest and most successful internet companies. Info Edge (its parent) listed in 2006 and has been a prolific early investor in Zomato and PolicyBazaar. Sanjeev personally invests with a patient, long-term outlook and deep understanding of Indian internet user behaviour.

name: Deepak Shahdadpuri
headline: Founder & MD, DSG Consumer Partners
current_role: Founder & Managing Director, DSG Consumer Partners
notable_exits_or_companies: Multiple consumer brand exits via DSG Consumer Partners across India and Southeast Asia
how_to_reach: DSG Consumer Partners application; LinkedIn; consumer brand founder community
investment_thesis: Authentic consumer brands with strong founder-brand fit; F&B innovation; health and wellness; beauty and personal care; emerging premium categories in India and Asia
bio: Deepak Shahdadpuri founded DSG Consumer Partners, Asia's premier early-stage consumer investment fund. He has backed some of India's most iconic consumer brands including Bira 91, Epigamia, and Vahdam Teas. Known for his deep relationships with founder-entrepreneurs and patient approach to consumer brand building.

name: Prashanth Prakash
headline: Founding Partner, Accel India | Partner since Fund I
notable_exits_or_companies: Accel India portfolio: Flipkart (earliest institutional investor), Swiggy, BrowserStack, Freshworks, Quikr
how_to_reach: Accel India (accel.com); warm intro via Accel portfolio preferred
investment_thesis: Founder-first partnerships; SaaS and enterprise software built from India for global markets; consumer internet with strong network effects; first-check to category leaders
bio: Prashanth Prakash is a founding partner of Accel India and has been with the firm since its inception. He led or co-led Accel's earliest investments in Flipkart and has been instrumental in building India's most successful VC franchise. Known for marathon-like patience with portfolio companies and exceptional founder relationships.

name: Karthik Reddy
headline: Managing Partner, Blume Ventures | Seed-Stage Pioneer
notable_exits_or_companies: Blume Ventures portfolio: Unacademy, Dunzo, Slice, Purplle, Niki.ai
how_to_reach: Blume Ventures application (blume.vc); warm intro via Blume portfolio network
investment_thesis: First institutional money in seed-stage Indian startups; contrarian bets; founders solving uniquely Indian problems; SaaS for global; consumer for Bharat
bio: Karthik Reddy co-founded Blume Ventures in 2010, creating India's first dedicated seed-stage venture fund. Blume has backed 100+ companies and created multiple unicorns. Known for being the first institutional check, Karthik is a builder of India's seed-stage ecosystem and an active community builder.

name: Mukul Arora
headline: Partner, SAIF Partners (Elevation Capital) | EdTech Champion
current_role: Managing Director, Elevation Capital (formerly SAIF Partners)
notable_exits_or_companies: Elevation portfolio exits: Paytm, MakeMyTrip, Just Dial (as platforms invested in)
how_to_reach: Elevation Capital application (elevationcapital.com); warm intro via Elevation network
investment_thesis: Large-scale EdTech opportunities; India consumer internet with solid unit economics; FinTech for mass market; category leaders with 10x revenue potential
bio: Mukul Arora is a Managing Director at Elevation Capital (formerly SAIF Partners India) and led the firm's marquee investment in Unacademy. He is India's foremost EdTech investor, with deep expertise across online learning, test preparation, and K12 segments. Known for his detailed diligence and long-term commitment to portfolio companies.

name: Vivek Paul
headline: Ex-CEO, Wipro Technologies | Global Tech Executive & Angel
current_role: Partner, TPG Capital; Board Member; Angel Investor
notable_exits_or_companies: Wipro Technologies (as CEO, grew from $100M to $1.3B revenue in 5 years)
how_to_reach: LinkedIn; warm intro via Wipro/TPG network; board connections
investment_thesis: Enterprise software and services with global delivery model; AI/ML infrastructure; healthcare technology; companies that can leverage India's engineering talent for global markets
known_investments: Various enterprise SaaS and deep tech early-stage companies
bio: Vivek Paul was CEO of Wipro Technologies during its most transformative decade, scaling global revenues from $100M to $1.3B. He then joined TPG Capital and serves on multiple boards. An active angel in enterprise tech and deep tech, he provides rare combination of global enterprise sales expertise and operational depth.

name: Sumir Chadha
headline: Co-founder & MD, WestBridge Capital
notable_exits_or_companies: WestBridge portfolio: MakeMyTrip (NASDAQ), Naspers India investments, various public market exits
how_to_reach: WestBridge Capital (westbridge.com); warm intro via WestBridge portfolio companies
investment_thesis: Growth-stage India opportunities; path to public market; companies with proven business models needing scale capital; crossover into public markets
bio: Sumir Chadha co-founded WestBridge Capital, one of India's largest growth-stage venture and crossover funds. WestBridge manages $3B+ in assets and focuses on Series B through pre-IPO rounds. Known for its disciplined investment approach and ability to identify category-leading businesses before their public market debut.
